title: Proclamation No. 256, s. 1964
text:
MALACAÑANG

RESIDENCE OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 256

RESERVING FOR BUILDING SITE PURPOSES OF THE DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE AND NATURAL RESOURCES A CERTAIN PARCEL OF LAND OF THE PRIVATE DOMAIN OF THE GOVERNMENT SITUATED IN THE CITY OF DAGUPAN, ISLAND OF LUZON

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Natural Resources and pursuant to the authority vested in me by law, I, Diosdado Macapagal, President of the Philippines, do hereby withdraw from sale or settlement and reserve for building site purposes under the administration of the Secretary of Agriculture and Natural Resources, subject to private rights, if any there be, a certain parcel of land of the private domain of the Government, situated in the City of Dagupan, Island of Luzon, and more particularly described as follows:

Lot 2 Bcs-2090 (Department of Agriculture & Natural Resources)

A parcel of land (Lot 2 of the consolidation and subdivision plan Bcs-2090, being a portion of the consolidation of Lots 3680-A, 3680-B, 3680-C, 3680-D, 3680-E, 3680-F, 3680-G, 3680-H, 3680-S, 3680-T, 3680-U, 3680-V, 3680-W, 3680-R, 3680-Q, 3680-P, 3680-O, 3680-N and 3680-Y (Port.) described on plan Psd-10000. LRC. Record No. …..), situated in the Poblacion, City of Dagupan. Bounded on the NW., along line 1-2, by Lot 1 of the consolidation and subdivision plan; on the NE., along lines 2-3-4-5-6-7-8, by Pantal River; on the SE., along lines 8-9-10-11-12, by Road; and on the S., along lines-12-13-14-15-16-17-1, by Torres Bugallon Avenue. Beginning at a point marked 1 on plan, being S. 81° 36′ E 162.80 meters from B.B.M. 6, Dagupan Cad., thence N. 12 o 56′ E., 67.18 meters to point 2; thence S. 77° 26′ E., 6.94 meters to point 3; thence S. 77 o 28′ E., 10.00 meters to point 4; thence S. 77 o 28′ E., 5.30 meters to point 5; thence S. 69° 47′ E., 4.74 meters to point 6; thence S. 69° 47′ E., 10.10 meters to point 7 thence S. 69° 47′ E., 10.10 meters to point 8; thence S., 12° 17′ W., 18.25 meters to point 9; thence S. 11° 55′ W., 2.03 meters to point 10; thence S. 12° 14′ W., 18.49 meters to point 11; thence S. 12° 15′ W., 20.00 meters to point 12; thence N. 83° 34′ W., 10.00 meters to point 13; thence N. 83° 34′ W., 10.00 meters to point 14; thence N. 83° 34′ W., 10.00 meters to point 15; thence N 82° 48′ W., 2.00 meters to point 16; thence N. 83° 34′ W., 10.00 meters to point 17; thence N. 83° 34′ W., 5.98 meters to the point of beginning; containing an area of 3,019 square meters. A11 points referred to are indicated on the plan and marked on the ground as follows: points 1 and 2 by B.L. Cyl. Conc. Mons. and the rest by Old B.L. Cyl. Conc. Mons.; bearings true; date of the consolidation and subdivision survey, Jan. 22-23, 1963 which was approved on June 24, 1963.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and I caused the seal of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 3rd day of June, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and sixty-four.

DIOSDADO MACAPAGAL President of the Philippines

By the President:

CALIXTO O. ZALDIVAR Acting Executive Secretary

Source : University of the Philippines, College of Law Library
